construct: Active construction of evaluated qualities vs. passive presentation of fixed traits
positive_pole: Treats herself, alliances, institutions, and acquisitions as instruments under construction calibrated to the structural requirements of the position to be occupied — language, religion, demeanor, intellectual capital, network capital, identity capital, even visual presentation engineered as outputs of a coordinated construction project rather than as inherited givens
negative_pole: Treats personal qualities, alliances, institutions, and acquisitions as fixed-trait inputs to be passively evaluated by external arbiters, with the operational task being the optimal display of pre-existing qualities rather than the engineered construction of new ones
behavioral_implication: When Catherine encounters a position whose requirements exceed her current qualities, she will undertake a coordinated construction project — language, religion, demeanor, intellectual capital, network capital — calibrated to the position's structural requirements, rather than either accepting the gap as fixed or attempting to redefine the position to fit her existing qualities. The disposition operates from the 1744 conversion preparation through the 1745–1762 network construction through the 1782 Bronze Horseman inscription, and is the operational foundation of every subsequent structural-asset construction in her career

construct: Long-arc structural-asset construction across low-leverage intervals vs. immediate-leverage opportunism
positive_pole: Invests heavily in structural-asset construction during low-leverage intervals when no specific deployment plan exists, treating construction as operationally meaningful even without proximate operational outlet, because specific operational openings will emerge whose precise timing and shape cannot be predicted in advance and the assets must be available when they emerge
negative_pole: Calibrates investment to the proximate operational opportunity, treating low-leverage intervals as periods to conserve resources and treating structural-asset construction without specific deployment plan as wasteful speculation
behavioral_implication: When Catherine has time without immediate operational opportunity (the seventeen years 1745–1762, the multi-decade Hermitage acquisitions, the Smolny human-capital construction, the long-arc Greek Project framework, the cultural-infrastructure construction across thirty-four years), she will invest in structural-asset construction calibrated to a class of possible future openings rather than to any specific predicted opening — accepting that most of the construction may never deploy operationally as the price of having the construction available when specific openings emerge

construct: Channel-bifurcated multi-audience instrument deployment vs. single-channel substantive optimization
positive_pole: Deploys the same substantive content (Nakaz, Enlightenment correspondence, religious toleration, cultural-acquisition) through multiple operational channels simultaneously, with the channel-bifurcated outcomes (European-reputational, domestic-deliberative, operational-intelligence, structural-credentialing) as the load-bearing variables — accepting substantive single-channel failure (the Legislative Commission produced no code) as operationally peripheral when the channel-bifurcated outcomes are achieved
negative_pole: Deploys content through a single dominant channel and optimizes for that channel's substantive outcome, treating the substantive single-channel result as the load-bearing variable and treating multi-audience reception as either coincidental byproduct or distracting complication
behavioral_implication: When Catherine designs a major instrument (Nakaz, Legislative Commission composition, Diderot library purchase, cultural-acquisitions, Charter to the Nobility), she will engineer the instrument's channel-bifurcated outcomes across multiple operational audiences simultaneously — accepting that the substantive single-channel outcome may fail visibly (the Commission produces no code, Diderot's recommendations are not implemented) as long as the channel-bifurcated outcomes (European reputation, operational intelligence, structural credentialing) are achieved

construct: Calibrated stability ceiling foreclosing reform under structural-stability threat vs. principled reform regardless of stability cost
positive_pole: Advances reform until reform threatens the structural conditions that permit continued rule, then forecloses operationally — pivoting from peasant reform after Pugachev, foreclosing Enlightenment positioning after the French Revolution, suppressing Radishchev and Novikov after 1789. The ceiling is set by political stability, not by the principle's logic, and the previously-articulated principles are sustained as performative-positioning until the environment shift makes their continued positioning operationally incompatible
negative_pole: Treats articulated principles as substantive commitments to be advanced even when their advancement threatens the structural conditions of continued rule, accepting structural-stability cost as the principled price of principled commitment
behavioral_implication: When Catherine's reform agenda encounters an operational-stability constraint (Pugachev's mobilization of peasant insurrection, the post-1789 European environment shift), she will foreclose the reform direction operationally while continuing the performative-positioning in any channel where the channel-bifurcation discipline still operates, and will eventually foreclose the performative-positioning itself when the environment shift makes its continued deployment operationally incompatible — sustaining the calibrated-stability-ceiling discipline as the load-bearing constraint on every reform agenda she pursues

construct: Coalition binding through structural cost-of-defection vs. coalition formation through shared values or personal trust
positive_pole: Engineers coalitions and structural alignments by identifying actors whose unfulfilled needs require coordination, installing herself as the coordinator, and designing the structural mechanism so that defection from the coalition costs each member more than continued participation — the Charter to the Nobility binds the noble class through privileges that depend on the regime's continuing operation, the Polish partitions bind Prussia and Austria through complicit-partner cost-distribution, the Russo-Austrian alignment binds Joseph II through reciprocal acquiescence in expansion
negative_pole: Builds alliances through personal-relationship cultivation, ideological alignment, or shared values, treating coalitions as products of trust rather than as products of structural cost-asymmetry
behavioral_implication: When Catherine needs a coalition (the noble class binding to the autocracy, the European-balance management for Black Sea expansion, the operational-stability framework for the post-Pugachev institutional architecture), she will engineer the structural mechanism through which defection costs exceed participation costs — privileges that depend on the regime, complicit partnerships that eliminate counter-coalition capacity, reciprocal acquiescence in mutual expansion — treating the structural cost-asymmetry as the load-bearing coalition-cohesion mechanism rather than the personal alignment of coalition members

construct: Composite institutional partnership integrating dimensions vs. compartmentalized authority across separate ministers
positive_pole: Integrates intimate, political-coordination, operational-administrative, and strategic-military dimensions in a single load-bearing operational partner (Potemkin), accepting the structural fragility of the integration (dependence on specific institutional partner, irreplaceability after the partner's death) as the operational price of force-multiplication through integration — the integrated partner provides operational reach that compartmentalized arrangements cannot match because the integration eliminates the coordination overhead between dimensions
negative_pole: Compartmentalizes operational functions across separate senior ministers in conventional monarchical practice, accepting coordination overhead between dimensions as the operational price of structural-resilience to any individual minister's loss
behavioral_implication: When Catherine needs operational coordination across multiple dimensions (the southern empire transformation, the Crimean integration, the Russo-Austrian alignment, the operational direction of the Second Russo-Turkish War), she will construct an integrated institutional partnership that combines the dimensions in a single load-bearing partner — accepting the structural fragility of the integration as the price of force-multiplication, and acknowledging that the partnership's irreplaceability will produce structural weakening when the partner is lost (the post-1791 reign's structural decline after Potemkin's death exemplifies the predicted failure mode)

construct: Personal authoring of load-bearing structural reforms vs. delegation to senior ministers or commissioned authors
positive_pole: Personally drafts the load-bearing structural reforms (Nakaz, provincial reform of 1775, Charter to the Nobility, major correspondence) at substantial operational cost in imperial time, treating the load-bearing structural reforms as requiring personal substantive engagement that no delegated drafting could match in structural quality or in integrated-architectural vision
negative_pole: Delegates structural-reform drafting to senior ministers or commissioned authors, treating the personal-authoring as an inefficient use of imperial time and treating the structural quality and integrated-architectural vision as achievable through senior delegation
behavioral_implication: When Catherine undertakes a load-bearing structural reform (the legal-philosophical synthesis of the Nakaz, the structural-architectural reconstruction of the provincial reform, the privilege-formalization architecture of the Charter to the Nobility, the major Enlightenment correspondence), she will personally draft the document despite the substantial imperial-time cost, treating the personal-authoring as operationally required for the structural-quality and integrated-architectural-vision outcomes that delegated drafting cannot match

construct: Calibrated state-management instrument deployment vs. principled uniform application of doctrinal commitments
positive_pole: Calibrates religious-policy, demographic-colonization, territorial-acquisition, cultural-acquisition by structural-utility in each specific case rather than by uniform application of doctrinal commitments — toleration extended to Old Believers because operational toleration produces structural integration, to Mennonites because attracts productive populations, to Jesuits because preserves operational educational infrastructure, to Crimean Tatars because integrates partition acquisitions; toleration withheld from Skoptsy because incompatible with imperial structure, restricted for Jews through Pale of Settlement
negative_pole: Applies doctrinal commitments uniformly across cases regardless of structural-utility differences, treating uniform application as the principled foundation of policy and treating case-by-case calibration as compromised application
behavioral_implication: When Catherine designs religious-policy, demographic-colonization, or cultural-policy (the Old Believer toleration, the Mennonite invitation, the Jesuit toleration after the papal suppression, the Volga German colonization), she will calibrate the structural privileges and the operational toleration by the structural-utility of the specific case rather than by uniform principled commitment — accepting the substantive contradiction between case-specific calibrations as operationally required by the calibrated-state-management discipline

construct: Engineered symbolic registration as operational instrument vs. symbolic registration as ceremonial formality
positive_pole: Engineers visual presentation, monument design, inscription text, and public ceremony as operational instruments calibrated for specific reception outcomes across multiple audiences — Preobrazhensky uniform of the Petrine era for the 1762 march, 'PETRO PRIMO CATHARINA SECUNDA' inscription on the Bronze Horseman, public purchase of the Diderot library with retention as paid librarian, the Mogilev personal-negotiation with Joseph II — treating the symbolic-registration as engineered operational instrument rather than as ceremonial formality
negative_pole: Treats symbolic registration as ceremonial formality whose details are administratively peripheral, delegating the design to senior advisers or accepting conventional defaults without engineered calibration
behavioral_implication: When Catherine constructs a public-symbolic instrument (the visual presentation of the 1762 coup leadership, the Diderot library purchase, the Falconet statue inscription, the cultural-acquisitions for the Hermitage), she will engineer the instrument's calibrated symbolic content — repudiation of Peter III's Prussian aesthetic through Petrine-era uniform, structural-credentialing through public retention of Diderot, institutional-successor positioning through 'PETRO PRIMO CATHARINA SECUNDA' — treating the symbolic-registration as an engineered operational instrument with specific reception outcomes across multiple audiences

construct: Operational priority over procedural-precedent cover at decision moments vs. procedural-precedent cover engineered at the moment of decision
positive_pole: Treats operational outcomes (Pasek-arrest immediate deployment, Peter III's death absorption, Pugachev suppression, Polish partitions) as decisively load-bearing relative to the procedural-precedent dimension at the moment of decision, deferring the management of long-arc procedural vulnerabilities to subsequent narrative-construction across the reign rather than attempting structural-cover at the moment of decision
negative_pole: Engineers the procedural-precedent cover at the moment of decision, accepting operational delay or opportunity cost as the price of foreclosing subsequent procedural challenges — treats long-arc procedural-precedent dimensions as load-bearing variables to be managed at the moment of decision rather than as deferrable subsequent-management tasks
behavioral_implication: When Catherine reaches a decision moment under operational pressure (the morning of June 28, 1762; the Ropsha death management; the Pugachev pivot; the succession-engineering project), she will prioritize the operational outcome over the procedural-precedent cover — accepting unresolved structural vulnerabilities (the unresolved Peter III death, the unresolved peasant-reform contradiction, the unresolved succession completion) as the operational price of the immediate operational outcome, with the long-arc procedural-precedent management deferred to subsequent narrative-construction. The pattern produces the operational successes the regime achieved and the structural vulnerabilities (Pugachev's impostor claim, the Pauline succession failure) that compounded across subsequent generations

construct: Continuous operational-intelligence collection through engineered networks vs. intelligence as discrete reporting from official channels
positive_pole: Maintains continuous operational-intelligence collection through engineered networks — the Enlightenment correspondence as European-intellectual intelligence, the Grimm correspondence as European-political intelligence, the Legislative Commission's 1,465 nakazi as Russian-domestic intelligence, the integrated Potemkin partnership as integrated operational intelligence, the diplomatic correspondence as European-diplomatic intelligence — with the network construction as the operational infrastructure through which intelligence is continuously collected rather than as discrete reporting from official channels
negative_pole: Relies on intelligence as discrete reporting from official channels (ambassadorial dispatches, ministerial reports, court intelligence services), treating intelligence-collection as a delegable function of the official apparatus rather than as personally-engineered network infrastructure
behavioral_implication: When Catherine constructs operational infrastructure (the Enlightenment correspondence networks, the Grimm correspondence as principal European-intellectual intelligence channel, the Legislative Commission's expanded composition as comprehensive opinion survey, the Mogilev personal-negotiation with Joseph II), she will engineer the infrastructure as continuous operational-intelligence collection rather than as discrete-reporting framework — accepting the substantial personal-time cost of the intelligence-collection infrastructure as the operational price of the continuously-current intelligence picture that delegated reporting cannot match

construct: Long-arc-structural-construction blindness to operational-completion deadlines vs. continuous deadline-detection across long-arc construction
positive_pole: (Failure mode pole.) Operates the long-arc structural-construction discipline effectively across most projects where the operational-completion deadline is operationally distant, but does not naturally generate the question 'what is the operational completion deadline that constrains this long-arc construction?' for projects where the deadline is constrained by life expectancy or by external environment shifts that the long-arc framework cannot anticipate — producing operational non-completion at the moment of unanticipated deadline expiration (the succession-engineering failure, the Greek Project's partial consummation, the post-1789 reactionary turn's incomplete management of the Enlightenment-positioning's structural reversal)
negative_pole: Continuously audits whether the structural-completion deadline is operationally proximate enough to require operational-completion at suboptimal procedural moments rather than continued deferral to optimal procedural moments, modifying the long-arc construction discipline when the deadline analysis identifies proximity
behavioral_implication: After Catherine has operated the long-arc structural-construction discipline successfully across multiple projects (cultural-infrastructure construction, institutional reforms, diplomatic architecture, demographic colonization), her characteristic deadline-detection caution will relax — and she will continue applying the long-arc construction-and-deferral pattern in projects whose operational-completion deadline is constrained by life expectancy or by environment-shift escalation (the succession-engineering project, the Greek Project, the post-1789 reactionary turn's calibration). The same operational-completion blindness pattern visible at Cicero's December 5, 63 BC decision and at Caesar's late-period structural-context blindness, applied to Catherine's specific failure mode of deferring operational completion in pursuit of optimal procedural moments that may not arrive within the available time horizon
